suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The kernel-auditor skill's gate shortcut (for verification-only features like final-gate) could be made a named alternative procedure ('Gate Verification Procedure') rather than requiring workers to report it as a deviation from the standard review flow. Workers correctly skip steps 3-6 for gate features per the Special Cases section, but then report followedProcedure: true with a deviation — making it a first-class named procedure would be cleaner.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: final-gate worker reported followedProcedure: true but listed a deviation for skipping steps 3-6, which the skill explicitly sanctions.

rejectedObservations.observation: Rng (xorshift64) struct is duplicated across 5+ test files. A shared test utility module could eliminate this duplication.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — The test architecture convention explicitly favors standalone files with #[path] imports. This is an accepted pattern documented in AGENTS.md test architecture section. Duplication is intentional to maintain test file independence.

rejectedObservations.observation: Host-side kernel model structs (ChannelTable, TimerTable, etc.) are duplicated across test files with no documentation of which file has the most complete/authoritative model.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented — Same accepted convention as the Rng duplication. The test architecture favors standalone files. Model evolution is slow (kernel APIs are stable post-audit) and the duplication risk is low. Not worth changing the established convention for.
